pages.text: Open the Crafting Terminal's $(thing)Fuel page$() with the third page button on the left rail. The rail then contains only the three page tabs. Use the $(thing)Fuel Target$() selector in the Energy Reserves header to choose Auto, Fuel, Brew Energy, or Bottle Energy; click to move forward, or Shift-click/scroll over it to move backward. Then place fuel in the dedicated input or Shift-click it.

$(thing)Installed Stations:$() Nine exact slots use two flow rows: Furnace, Blast Furnace, Smoker, Campfire, Brewing Stand, Crafting Table, Stonecutter, Smithing Table, and one compatible axe. The first five generate energy at their stack count per tick; the final four only unlock recipes/actions. All stay installed across page changes and menu close and can be Shift-clicked back.

$(thing)Runtime Fuel:$() Any item with a positive current runtime burn time is accepted. Each item adds exactly that many Fuel ticks, so logs, datapack fuels, and compatible modded fuels work without a Magic Storage whitelist. Container remainders such as the empty Lava Bucket are returned, and overflow rejects the whole conversion.

$(thing)Brew Energy:$() Blaze Rod $(thing)1,200$(); Blaze Powder $(thing)600$().$(br)$(thing)Bottle Energy:$() Glass Bottle or Potion $(thing)1$().$(br2)Auto prefers the compatible pool with fewer distinct suppliers, then lower stored total, then stable order. A Blaze Rod therefore fills Brew Energy under Auto. The Storage page stores machines and fuel normally.
